DISCOVERY 302, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR ORDER FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND STRIKING CLAIM IS GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: CHARLES O. GEERHART; CLERK L. FONG; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)
PROOF OF SERVICE OF ORDER STRIKING THE CLAIM OF JAY JOHNSON 08-099 (TRANSACTION ID # 57985368) FILED BY PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA

DISCOVERY 302, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO FIRST SET OF DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION AND INSPECTION OF DOCUMENTS; PETITIONER'S FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES PETITIONER'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. WHILE THE MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION STATES THAT "[T]O DATE, NO RESPONSES HAVE BEEN RECEIVED FROM CLAIMANT," THAT IS NOT WHAT THE SUPPORTING DECLARATION SAYS. IN THE DECLARATION, COUNSEL REPRESENTS ONLY THAT "[T]O DATE, PETITIONER HAS NOT RECEIVED COMPLETE ANSWERS TO THESE DISCOVERY REQUESTS." DECLARATION OF PAMELA A. UNDERWOOD AT PARA. 3, EMPHASIS ADDED. BECAUSE THE SUBSTANTIVE AND PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS FOR MOTIONS COMPELLING RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY DIFFER, DEPENDING UPON WHETHER INADEQUATE ANSWERS WERE SERVED, OR NO ANSWERS AT ALL WERE PROVIDED, AND BECAUSE THE PAPERS SUBMITTED IN SUPPORT OF THIS MOTION ARE IN CONFLICT ON THIS ISSUE, THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. COURT TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: RICHARD GROTCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Answers To First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Counsel Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Feb-27-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: PAMELA UNDERWOOD, ASSISTANT DISTRICT ATTORNEY, OFFICE OF THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY, 415.551.9544, FOR PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA; NO OTHER APPEARANCES.

RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's motion To Compel Answers To First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Counsel Form Interrogatories IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM ERNST HALPERIN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioner'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Oct-03-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Joy Johnson, Tel. 415.819.5594, claimant, appearing on his own behalf;
No other appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Unopposed Motion To Compel Answers To First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioner's First Set Of Special Interrogatories Petitioner's unopposed motion to compel is DENIED without prejudice. While the memorandum in support of the motion states that "[t]o date, no responses have been received from Claimant," that is not what the supporting declaration says. In the declaration, counsel represents only that "[t]o date, Petitioner has not received complete answers to these discovery requests." Declaration of Pamela A. Underwood at para. 3, emphasis added. Because the substantive and procedural requirements for motions compelling responses to discovery differ, depending upon whether inadequate answers were served, or no answers at all were provided, and because the papers submitted in support of this motion are in conflict on this issue, the motion is DENIED without prejudice. Court to prepare order. Judge Pro Tem: Richard Grotch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported
